articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Fears the Iran war could lead to UK supermarket shortages this summer and news of big oil windfalls lead Thursday's papers.
articles.title: Newspaper headlines: 'Summer of shortages' and 'War windfall'

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: In the third part of an undercover investigation, the BBC reveals how rules aimed at protecting abuse victims are being exploited.
articles.title: Migrants are making false domestic abuse allegations to stay in the UK, BBC investigation finds

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: About 22,000 students in England were told they were given loans by mistake and must immediately pay the money back.
articles.title: Nine universities start legal action over student loan error row

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: During their visit down under, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex have chosen to learn more about First Nations Australians' history.
articles.title: Harry and Meghan join Aboriginal walking tour in Melbourne

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: From heat pumps to offshore wind, the UK’s net zero push is facing growing scrutiny. Are rising costs undermining climate goals?
articles.title: Why some argue cutting costs is the best way to cut carbon

articles.author: BBC Sport
articles.description: The quickest strike in this season's Champions League, seven goals, two red cards and the competition's 15-time winners exiting in anger - once again the knockout stages of European club football's elite tournament has delivered drama in spades.
articles.title: Bayern Munich vs Real Madrid: Giants deliver Champions League classic

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Teachers say more children are starting school without skills like basic communication and potty training.
articles.title: The city where primary school offers come with a toilet-training guide

articles.description: Top executives from firms such as Meta and YouTube will be asked what they are doing to protect children.
articles.title: Starmer calls Downing Street meeting with social media bosses

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Investigations found Roo had been accidentally poisoned with a dose of vitamin D prescribed for growing pains.
articles.title: I feared my son had a brain tumour but he'd been poisoned with vitamin D

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.
articles.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
